app.subtitle: Online course
app.heroTitle: Learn React from fundamentals to architecture

practice.title: Mini practice
practice.description: After studying this module, try to explain the concept without looking at the code.
items: Identify which data should be props.

questions.question: What causes React to re-render a component?
When is it a good idea to use global state?
options: When data is shared across many areas

intro.title: React Fundamentals
intro.description: What React is, JSX, components, and how to think in declarative UI.

lessons.title: What is React?
lessons.content: React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces using reusable components. Its main idea is to describe how the UI should look based on the current application state.

lessons.title: JSX
lessons.content: JSX lets you write HTML-like structure inside JavaScript. It is transformed into React calls to create elements.

components.title: Components and Props
components.description: How to split interfaces into small, reusable, and maintainable pieces.

lessons.title: Components
lessons.content: A component is a function that returns UI. The key is to separate responsibilities: headers, cards, forms, lists, buttons, etc.

lessons.title: Props
lessons.content: Props are data a component receives from its parent. They help customize components without duplicating code.

state.title: State and Events
state.description: Handling user interaction using useState and events.

lessons.title: useState
lessons.content: useState stores information that can change over time. When state changes, React re-renders the component.

lessons.title: Events
lessons.content: React uses events such as onClick, onChange and onSubmit. They usually update state or trigger business logic.

effects.title: Effects and Lifecycle
effects.description: When to use useEffect to synchronize with external systems.

lessons.title: useEffect
lessons.content: useEffect is used to synchronize the component with something external: APIs, subscriptions, storage, timers or browser events.

lessons.title: Cleanup
lessons.content: When an effect creates a subscription, timer or listener, it is good practice to clean up the resource to avoid memory leaks.

routing.title: Routing
routing.description: Concepts of routes, pages, navigation and dynamic parameters.

lessons.title: Routes
lessons.content: In an SPA, routing changes the view without reloading the entire page. Frameworks like Next.js use file-based routing; React Router uses route configuration.

state-management.title: Global State
state-management.description: Context, Zustand, Redux and when to use each option.

lessons.title: Local vs global state
lessons.content: Local state lives inside a component. Global state is shared across multiple screens or components: authenticated user, cart, theme, preferences, etc.

lessons.title: When to scale
lessons.content: Not everything needs Redux or Zustand. Start with local state; if many components need the same data, consider Context or a global store.
